top_signals.title: US Stocks Rally on Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz; SpaceX Gains
top_signals.summary: US stocks rose sharply after the US and Iran reached an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices lower and easing concern about inflation ahead of Kevin Warsh’s first meeting as chairman of the Federal Reserve this week. SpaceX shares advanced 20%, helping boost risk-on sentiment.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Iran war in seven charts
top_signals.summary: From oil markets to reconstruction, the fallout from the conflict will unfold over years
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Worst of US Inflation May Be Over If Iran Peace Deal Sticks
top_signals.summary: The peace agreement between the US and Iran, if it holds, suggests the worst of the war-driven inflation has likely passed, but the outlook for American consumers and the broader economy remains far from certain.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Peak oil demand arrives sooner than expected
top_signals.summary: Amid the disruptions caused by the US-Israeli-Iran war, countries have begun adapting to life with less oil.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: How the Strait of Hormuz reopening could unfold if the U.S.-Iran deal is implemented
top_signals.summary: Tanker traffic through Hormuz could quickly increase but it is unclear whether transits will approach prewar levels, according to Kpler.
